name: Sopapilla Cheesecake Pie
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Generously grease a 9x9-inch baking dish with cooking spray or butter.

2. In a mixing bowl, beat the Cream Cheese, some White Sugar, and Vanilla Extract together with an electric mixer on medium speed until the mixture is completely smooth and creamy with no lumps.

3. Unroll half of the Refrigerated Pizza Dough onto a clean work surface. Use a rolling pin to gently roll and shape it into a 9x9-inch square. Carefully press this dough into the bottom of the prepared baking dish, making sure it reaches all corners and edges.

4. Evenly spread the cream cheese mixture over the dough layer, smoothing it with a spatula to create a uniform filling.

5. Roll out the remaining pizza dough into another 9x9-inch square and carefully place it over the cream cheese layer, pressing the edges to seal.

6. In a small bowl, use a fork to mash together the remaining White Sugar, softened Butter, and Cinnamon until the mixture is crumbly and well combined. Dot this cinnamon butter mixture evenly over the top layer of dough.

7. Bake in the preheated oven until the pizza dough has puffed up beautifully and turned golden brown, about 30 minutes. The top should be crispy and fragrant.

8. Remove from the oven and immediately drizzle the Honey generously over the hot dessert. Allow the cheesecake to cool completely in the pan for about 2 hours to set properly before cutting into squares and serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven and grease a 9x9-inch baking dish.

2. Beat the Cream Cheese, some White Sugar, and Vanilla Extract until smooth and creamy.

3. Press half the Refrigerated Pizza Dough into the bottom of the dish.

4. Spread the cream cheese mixture evenly over the dough.

5. Cover with the remaining pizza dough layer.

6. Top with a crumbly mixture of remaining White Sugar, Butter, and Cinnamon.

7. Bake until puffed and golden, then drizzle with Honey.

8. Cool completely before cutting into squares.
